title: Prompt Security, Untrusted Context, Privacy, and Authorization · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Harden a tool-using prompt that processes untrusted documents and external results. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a prompt threat model and red-team suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate trusted instructions from untrusted data and label authority boundaries.
Recognize direct and indirect prompt injection and prevent authority escalation.
Exclude secrets and irrelevant proprietary context and minimize tool privileges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Instruction Data Separation · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt security injection. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt security injection. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Instruction Data Separation without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate trusted instructions from untrusted data and label authority boundaries.

content.text: Separate trusted instructions from untrusted data and label authority boundaries. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt security injection. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Instruction Data Separation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a prompt threat model and red-team suite.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Text inside a retrieved document is safe to follow as instruction. This fails the Instruction Data Separation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompting Best Practices (Current Claude model guidance) — Controls clear instructions, examples, structure, reasoning, tools, and model-specific migration cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate trusted instructions from untrusted data and label authority boundaries.

stimulus.title: Red Team Maintenance case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt security injection. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A one-time red-team exercise permanently secures a prompt.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change. Artifact under review: a prompt threat model and red-team suite.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Red Team Maintenance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Red Team Maintenance applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.
